summary: 10 concrete products and tools surfaced from HN launches and comment recommendations.

items.name: Workweave Router
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Smart model routing directly in Claude, Codex and Cursor
items.summary: Model router for Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor.
items.why_included: HN liked the idea of routing each coding task to the cheapest useful model.
items.evidence: The launch post says it routes coding-agent requests to faster or stronger models as needed, and HN comments focused on token savings, caching, and preserving agent feedback loops.

items.name: Flue
items.source_story_title: MicroVMs: Run isolated sandboxes with full lifecycle control
items.summary: Open agent framework with sandboxes, tools, and subagents.
items.why_included: It surfaced as a concrete sandbox alternative in the MicroVMs thread.
items.evidence: A commenter called Flue another player in this space and pointed to its interactive product for sandboxed agents and workflows.

items.name: MicroVMs
items.source_story_title: MicroVMs: Run isolated sandboxes with full lifecycle control
items.summary: AWS Lambda microVM sandboxes with lifecycle control.
items.why_included: Strong infra launch with a clear security and isolation angle.
items.evidence: HN compared it with hosted sandbox providers and local libkrun-style setups, making the microVM use case concrete.

items.name: WisdPi 10G Ethernet Expansion Card
items.source_story_title: Framework's 10G Ethernet module exposes USB-C's complexity
items.summary: 10GbE expansion card for Framework laptops and Desktop.
items.why_included: Useful hardware niche with real USB-C bandwidth tradeoffs.
items.evidence: Comments dug into the Gen 2x2 bottleneck and repeatedly compared it with Frameworks regular Ethernet card.

items.name: WebBase-III
items.source_story_title: Show HN: WebBase-III – dBASE III rebuilt in the browser with its own interpreter
items.summary: Browser-based dBASE III revival with its own interpreter.
items.why_included: Nostalgia-heavy project with a working demo and strong response.
items.evidence: Commenters praised the dot prompt, one-click Codespaces tryout, and the dBASE/Clipper/FoxPro workflow.

items.name: barcode.new
items.summary: In-browser barcode generator frontend with vector output.
items.why_included: A comment-surfaced tool that solves a mundane but real workflow pain.
items.evidence: One commenter plugged barcode.new because most online barcode tools either lack vector output or push code through servers.

items.name: Sunshine
items.source_story_title: My Steam Machine is a 50ft HDMI cable
items.summary: Self-hosted game-stream host for Moonlight clients.
items.why_included: A practical recommendation from a high-signal home streaming setup thread.
items.evidence: A commenter said game streaming over Ethernet is hard to beat and called Sunshine the host they run.

items.name: MoonDeck
items.source_story_title: My Steam Machine is a 50ft HDMI cable
items.summary: Steam Deck plugin that simplifies Moonlight game sessions.
items.why_included: A concrete helper layer for the Sunshine/Moonlight stack.
items.evidence: HN praised it as the automation layer that makes launching Steam games through Moonlight much less fiddly.

items.name: SwiftLaTeX
items.summary: Browser-native LaTeX engines running in WASM.
items.why_included: Practical browser TeX with real curiosity about offline use.
items.evidence: Comments asked for an offline, curated distribution and debated how far WASM layout can go.

items.name: You're the OS
items.source_story_title: A game where you're an OS and have to manage processes, memory and I/O events
items.summary: Game where you manage processes, memory, and I/O as an OS.
items.why_included: Strong systems-game concept with clear educational upside.
items.evidence: HN wanted scripting, a deeper tech tree, and even OS-class use; several called the idea fun and impressive.
